stories.original_summary: A newly identified ichthyosaur from the UK’s Jurassic Coast is rewriting part of the prehistoric playbook. Nicknamed the “Sword Dragon of Dorset,” the three-meter-long marine reptile lived during a poorly understood window of evolution when major ichthyosaur groups were disappearing and new ones eme
stories.fantasy_headline: Ancient Sword Dragon Bones Uncovered on Dorset Shore!
stories.fantasy_story: Deep on the Dorset Shore, seekers have unearthed the ancient remains of a creature known as the 'Sword Dragon.' This three-meter-long beast, a type of sea serpent, changes what we thought we knew about the deep-sea creatures of old. Its discovery reveals forgotten lore about the great beasts that swam the waters long ago.

stories.original_headline: Scientists engineer bacteria to eat cancer tumors from the inside out
stories.original_summary: Researchers are engineering bacteria to invade tumors and consume them from the inside. Because tumor cores lack oxygen, they’re the perfect breeding ground for these microbes. The team added a genetic tweak that helps the bacteria survive longer near oxygen-exposed edges — but only once enough of t
stories.fantasy_headline: Alchemists Enchant Tiny Spirits to Devour Dark Growths!
stories.fantasy_story: The Alchemists' Guild has unveiled a new, potent enchantment. They've crafted tiny, living spirits capable of invading and devouring the blight of dark growths from within. These magical microbes thrive where the blight is thickest, offering a new hope against cursed flesh.
